card.intuition: Adds a third sublayer to each encoder block: masked self-attention (causal mask prevents position i attending to j > i), cross-attention (queries from decoder, keys/values from encoder), then FFN. Causal masking enables autoregressive generation: at each step the model conditions on all previously generated tokens but not future ones.
card.analogy: A translator reading sentence-by-sentence: at each word they've already written (masked self-attention) they glance back at the source text (cross-attention), then compose the next word (FFN + projection). The mask enforces left-to-right generation — peeking at future words would be cheating.
card.time_complexity: O(N · T² · d) training (parallelised); O(N · T · d) per token at inference (KV cache)
card.space_complexity: O(N · T · d) KV cache at inference
when_to_use: Any task where output depends on both input and previously generated tokens
gotchas: KV cache: cache past K,V to avoid recomputing at each autoregressive step — critical for inference speed
Causal mask must be applied in masked self-attention, not cross-attention
Temperature at inference: scale logits by 1/T before softmax — T<1 sharpens, T>1 flattens distribution

exercise.prompt: Build a causal attention mask for autoregressive decoding.
hints: Future positions get -∞ so that after softmax they become 0 (exp(-∞) = 0), effectively blocking attention to those positions.
exercise.test_code:
import math
mask = causal_mask(4)
# Lower-triangular structure
for i in range(4):
    for j in range(4):
        if j <= i:
            assert mask[i][j] == 0.0, f'mask[{i}][{j}] should be 0'
        else:
            assert mask[i][j] == float('-inf'), f'mask[{i}][{j}] should be -inf'

# Applying mask: future scores become -inf
scores = [[1.0]*4 for _ in range(4)]
masked = apply_causal_mask(scores, mask)
assert masked[0][1] == float('-inf')
assert masked[2][1] == 1.0   # past position allowed
assert masked[3][3] == 1.0   # diagonal allowed
print('PASS')
